title: EP064 - Steve Waterhouse : La guerre numérique a commencé
summary: Dans cet épisode de Sans Limite, Hugo reçoit Steve Waterhouse, spécialiste en cybersécurité et l’un des premiers cyber-soldats au Canada. Ancien membre du Royal 22e Régiment, Steve raconte comment il a troqué son arme pour un clavier au moment où l’armée canadienne entrait dans l’ère numérique. De l’infanterie aux premiers réseaux informatiques des Forces armées canadiennes, il a été au cœur de la modernisation technologique, tant à Montréal qu’au Collège militaire royal de Saint-Jean. On parle de cybercrime, d’IA, de mots de passe, de surconnexion, de vulnérabilité collective… et de l’importance de rester le plus analogue possible dans un monde obsédé par le numérique. Un échange lucide, accessible et percutant avec un homme qui a vu venir les menaces… bien avant tout le monde. On fait ça SANS LIMITE.  Hébergé par Acast. Visitez acast.com/privacy pour plus d'informations.

title: EP062 - Babu : La voix d’une  génération
summary: Dans cet épisode de Sans Limite, Hugo reçoit Babu, une icône de MusiquePlus, un gars de radio dans l’âme et un survivant. De son enfance en région, solitaire mais déjà obsédé par la radio, à l’époque mythique de MusiquePlus où tout était possible, Babu revient sur les plus belles années d’une télévision libre, live et sans filet. Il raconte aussi l’accident de moto qui aurait dû lui coûter la vie, les séquelles, la reconstruction et ce sentiment de vivre depuis sur du temps emprunté. Entre drift, radio, chutes brutales et retours inattendus, Babu parle vrai, sans filtre, avec lucidité et gratitude. On fait ça SANS LIMITE!  Hébergé par Acast. Kareem opens up about his journey founding The Tech Effect and how his unique approach to bridging the gap between untapped talent and opportunity in both corporate and entrepreneurial spaces goes beyond just teaching technical digital skills to also addressing mindset and overcoming the hidden blockers to growth.  Together, they explore the differences between mentorship and coaching, and how clarity, courage, and a supportive network can help you step into your potential while staying connected to your core values. You’ll also hear insights on building confidence, taking healthy risks, and the importance of “sending the elevator back down” to support those coming up behind you.  If you’re seeking clarity on your next steps, curious about leveraging technology for good, or simply looking for permission to craft your career and life by design, this episode is filled with actionable insights and wholehearted encouragement. title: EP061 - Bernard Drainville : Devenir premier ministre?
summary: Dans cet épisode de Sans Limite, Hugo reçoit Bernard Drainville. Correspondant en Amérique latine, il a couvert la guerre civile en Colombie, la crise en Argentine et a même été détenu par la guérilla. Des expériences marquantes qui ont façonné sa vision du monde. Il fait ensuite le saut en politique avec le Parti Québécois avant de revenir en 2022 sous la Coalition Avenir Québec. Ministre de l’Éducation puis ministre de l’Environnement, il défend ses réformes : interdiction du cellulaire en classe, mesures sur la laïcité, financement des partis politiques, immigration et modernisation de l’État. En 2026, il se lance dans la course à la chefferie de la CAQ. Un échange franc, stratégique et humain. On fait ça… SANS LIMITE  Hébergé par Acast. Visitez acast.com/privacy pour plus d'informations.

title: Ep 49: Stem Cell Doctor on Aging Backwards and Full Body Rejuvenation
summary: So much of modern “anti-aging” is built on fear - fear of wrinkles, fear of decline, fear that your best years are behind you. But what if aging isn’t just something to fight, but something to understand? In this episode of Grounded Wellness, Bethany McDaniel sits down with Dr. Joy Kong (UCLA-trained, triple board-certified stem cell specialist) to demystify regenerative medicine and reframe what stem cells actually are: not magic, not science fiction - but part of the original intelligence of life itself. Dr. Joy takes us all the way back to the beginning - the single stem cell that became you - and explains how stem cells work in the body today, why they matter for repair, and what’s real versus hype in the rapidly growing stem cell world. From autoimmune conditions and neurological recovery to skin rejuvenation, hair health, and even fertility markers, she unpacks what she’s seen clinically, what research suggests, and where language matters when it comes to FDA-approved claims. Bethany also asks the questions most people are thinking (but don’t know how to ask): What’s the difference between autologous vs. allogeneic stem cells? How are umbilical cord-derived cells sourced and screened? What are the risks? How do critics misunderstand the research? And can supplements or therapies really “mobilize” your body’s own stem cells - or does that just burn through your supply faster? This conversation isn’t just about stem cells. It’s about what it means to heal, to repair, and to reclaim agency over your health with wisdom, discernment, and a long-term view of the body. In this episode, we cover:  What stem cells actually are (and why the “hype” exists)The “gold standard” stem cells being used today: mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs)How MSCs “trace signals” and support the body’s repair processesAutologous vs. allogeneic cells - and why source matters as we ageWhat umbilical cord donor screening looks like (and what ethical sourcing means)Why stem cell claims are complicated in the U.S. (FDA language + approvals)What the research suggests stem cells may support across organ systemsExosomes vs. stem cells: what’s different, what lasts longer, what’s more targetedStem cell “mobilizers” (exercise, hyperbaric oxygen, supplements): helpful or risky?Skin, hair, and aesthetic regeneration - what actually works and whyPeptides, sauna, and other longevity tools Dr. Joy uses clinically Explore Primally Pure: Shop Clean Skincare: https://bit.ly/4e1HzVv Learn More from Dr. Joy Kong: Website: joykongmd.comBook: Tiger of Beijing: on AmazonYoutube: Joy Kong MD ChannelPodcast: The Dr Joy Kong PodcastInstagram: @dr_joy_kong Follow Primally Pure: Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/primallypureTikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@primallypureYouTube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCHLEirBmkKP7jGx4vF05oRAPrimally Pure App: https://primallypure.onelink.me/jJun/et56748s

title: Reclaiming Agency Over Your Mind and Body with Courtney Townley
summary: In this episode of Life by Design, Dr. Robyne welcomes health and self-leadership coach, author, speaker, and podcast host Courtney Townley for a refreshingly honest conversation about the noise and pressure that often surround modern wellness culture. Together, they explore why chasing one-size-fits-all solutions and holding ourselves to unrealistic standards so often leaves us feeling frustrated, disconnected, and farther from true well-being.  This conversation offers a more human way forward: one rooted in authentic self-awareness, radical responsibility, and compassionate curiosity. From the power of “Isn’t that interesting?” as an antidote to self-judgment to the importance of setting healthy boundaries (even when it feels uncomfortable), they share practical insights and small, accessible practices for tuning in, soothing your nervous system, and honoring what you need.  If you’re ready to reclaim your sense of agency, experiment with supportive practices, and explore what genuinely feels nourishing for you, this conversation will meet you exactly where you are. title: EP059 - Olivier Aubin-Mercier : Frapper pour 1M$
summary: Dans cet épisode de Sans Limite, Hugo Girard reçoit Olivier Aubin-Mercier, ancien combattant de MMA et double champion du monde de la PFL. De ses débuts en judo inspirés par Nicolas Gill, à l’UFC où il est entré trop tôt avec presque aucune expérience, Olivier raconte avec une honnêteté rare les hauts, les doutes et la pression du plus gros circuit au monde. Il explique pourquoi la PFL a tout changé : le format tournoi, l’esprit d’équipe, la quête du « boss final »… et la victoire qui lui a valu un million de dollars. Un échange posé, intelligent et profondément humain avec un gars qui a compris que gagner ne veut pas toujours dire continuer. On fait ça SANS LIMITE!  Hébergé par Acast. title: Doing the Hard Thing Today to Avoid the Harder Thing Tomorrow with Rory Vaden
summary: In today’s episode, Dr. Robyne welcomes author, personal branding strategist, and entrepreneur Rory Vaden for a practical and inspiring conversation about what it really takes to create lasting change, both for ourselves and for the people we serve. Together, they explore why the highest performers don’t chase shortcuts but instead commit to doing the difficult, meaningful work others often avoid. Rory shares his “take the stairs” philosophy, reminding us that real progress comes from consistently choosing long-term results over immediate comfort.  You’ll hear why enjoyment isn’t a requirement for meaningful action, how the “pain paradox” reveals that tough choices today often create an easier future, and why unglamorous, boring habits can radically transform your life. They also discuss why hope isn’t just a soft skill but a tactical advantage, and how shifting your thinking from “Is it possible?” to “How could it be possible?” unlocks new pathways and opportunities.  If you’re ready to redefine your habits, design your future with intention, or find the courage to share your story, this episode is your invitation to think long-term, commit to the work, embrace the messy middle, and trust that every setback is preparing you for something greater.  Listener note: This conversation includes mention of suicide. If this topic feels sensitive for you, please take care while listening.  In this episode, you’ll learn:  • The four levels of influence and how understanding them can multiply your impact  • Why the highest performers aren’t chasing shortcuts  • The “pain paradox” and how embracing tough moments now leads to easier, more fulfilled living later  • How hope is both a strategy and a superpower for leadership, personal growth, and resilience  • Tactical mindset shifts to help you ask better questions and unlock new possibilities  • Why you’re most powerfully positioned to serve the person you once were  • What it truly means to unlearn limitation, reprogram your thinking, and step into identities you once thought impossible  ***   Dr. Robyne’s new book, I Hope So, is now available! title: Ep 48: The Hidden Switch Controlling Your Weight, Skin, and Energy w/ Candi Frazier
summary: Most women don’t want to lose weight just to hit a number on the scale. They want to feel confident, attractive, and comfortable in their own body again. And yet for so many women, even eating clean and working out feels like it’s not enough. In this episode of Grounded Wellness, Bethany McDaniel sits down with functional nutritionist and Primal Bod founder Candi Frazier - a woman whose own health journey led her to uncover what most weight-loss conversations completely miss: insulin, metabolism, and the hidden signals that decide whether your body stores fat or burns it. Candi shares how years of “doing everything right” - clean eating, calorie restriction, intense workouts, keto, paleo, fasting - still left her stuck in a body that wouldn’t respond. The breakthrough didn’t come from more discipline. It came from understanding insulin, blood sugar, and how women’s bodies actually work - especially under chronic stress. Together, Bethany and Candi unpack why so many women feel trapped in cycles of hypoglycemia, cravings, stubborn weight gain, hormonal chaos, and burnout-and how those symptoms often show up long before a lab test ever flags a problem. They explore why insulin resistance can exist even in women who look “healthy,” how visceral fat drives inflammation and aging, and why most mainstream nutrition advice unintentionally keeps women stuck. This conversation challenges nearly every modern wellness narrative - from constant snacking and “healthy” paleo treats to aggressive fasting, overtraining, and the belief that fat loss is about willpower. Instead, it reframes fat as information, hunger as a signal, and metabolism as something that can be rebuilt - not punished. If you’ve ever felt like your body is working against you, this episode will change how you think about food, movement, fasting, hormones, and what it actually means to be healthy. ﻿﻿ In this episode, we explore: Why insulin, not calories, is the master switch for fat lossHow hypoglycemia, cravings, and “hanger” signal deeper metabolic issuesWhy keto and paleo often fail women (even when done “right”)The difference between visceral fat vs. protective fat - and why it mattersHow insulin resistance shows up on the skin before labs catch itWhy more fasting isn’t always better (especially for women)Protein, muscle, and finding your personal “Goldilocks zone”Why meat-first eating stabilizes hormones, appetite, and energyHow movement, walking, and post-meal activity improve insulin sensitivityThe difference between pleasure, joy, and long-term healthThis is an honest, no-nonsense conversation about metabolism, modern food environments, and reclaiming agency over your body - without extremes, starvation, or shame. title: Ep 47: Why Pain Doesn’t Go Away (Even After Your Body Heals) w/ Vinny Crispino
summary: Most of us only think about movement when something hurts. But what if the stiffness you’ve been ignoring is actually your body’s early warning system - not a life sentence, but information? In this episode of Grounded Wellness, Bethany McDaniel sits down with Vinny Crispino (Pain Academy) - an eight-time All-American athlete whose life changed after a surfing accident left him with a broken back and years of relentless, chronic pain. What followed wasn’t a simple “rehab protocol,” but decade-long unraveling of what pain really is - and why so many people stay stuck even after tissue heals. ﻿﻿﻿ Vinny breaks down the missing link most pain conversations skip: the nervous system. He shares why chasing a “perfect” squat, the “right” posture, or the one miracle stretch can keep you trapped in an endless loop - and how healing often begins when we stop trying to outthink the body and start learning to feel safe inside it again. Together, Bethany and Vinny explore why pain isn’t always a structural problem, why rest and ice shouldn’t be the long-term strategy, and how tiny “micro-doses” of movement throughout the day can re-train your body far more effectively than heroic bursts of workouts followed by hours of sitting. They also dive into the mindset shift that changes everything: meeting sensation with curiosity instead of fear - because the meaning we attach to pain can be the very thing that keeps it alive. This conversation is equal parts practical and deeply human. Whether you’re rebuilding from injury, stuck in chronic pain, or simply trying to futureproof your body for a long, capable life - this one will change how you think about movement, healing, and your relationship with your body. In this episode, we explore: Why squatting is a “position of rest” - and what it reveals about mobility + balanceThe truth about knees over toes and why rigid movement rules can backfireHow pain can become chronic even after the body healsWhat’s happening in the brain when fear plus pain become a loopWhy “rest + ice” shouldn’t be the strategy (and what to do instead)The difference between stretching what’s tight vs. moving what’s tightMicro-dosing movement: simple ways to move more without overhauling your lifeVinny’s “minimum standard of movement” (and why it’s the key to consistency) Explore Primally Pure: Shop Clean Skincare: https://bit.ly/4e1HzVv Learn More from Vinny Crispino: Website: https://painacademy.net/Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/painacademy/ Follow Primally Pure: Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/primallypureTikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@primallypureYouTube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCHLEirBmkKP7jGx4vF05oRA

title: Ep. 46: Why Women Feel Off And What’s Really Causing It With Dr. Austin Lake
summary: So many women are doing all the “right” things - eating clean, working out, staying busy - and yet still feel tired, inflamed, foggy, and stuck in a body that doesn’t respond. The frustration isn’t laziness. It’s often a body that’s been running on stress for too long. In this episode of Grounded Wellness, Bethany McDaniel sits down with Dr. Austin Lake, a functional medicine practitioner who helps women uncover the quiet, compounding drivers behind fatigue, stubborn weight gain, hormone imbalance, and burnout. Instead of chasing quick fixes, Dr. Lake points us back to something both ancient and surprisingly overlooked: our daily rhythms - how we wake, eat, move, and rest in alignment with the sun. What starts as a conversation about circadian rhythm becomes a bigger invitation: to rebuild the body’s foundation through consistent sleep, morning light, steady nourishment, and a reduced toxic load - not as another “protocol,” but as a way to restore calm, clarity, and resilience from the inside out. Together, Bethany and Dr. Lake explore why modern wellness trends can sometimes backfire for women - like skipping breakfast, fasting too aggressively, overtraining, and leaning on caffeine to power through the day. They also discuss hidden stressors that can keep women feeling stuck (like mold exposure, hormone-disrupting toxins, and impaired thyroid conversion), and why true healing often requires consistency more than intensity. This conversation is a reminder that vitality isn’t built in extremes. It’s built in small, repeatable decisions - the kind that bring the nervous system back to safety and the body back to balance. In this episode, we explore: Why so many women feel burnt out even when they’re “healthy”How cortisol, meal timing, and circadian rhythm shape hormones + metabolismThe hidden reasons weight loss can feel impossible (even with effort)Why “first get healthy, then get fit” matters - especially for womenDetox, thyroid function, and reducing daily toxic burdenDr. Lake’s most practical first step for feeling better: set a bedtime alarm Biblical References (NIV translations): Genesis 1:29 Then God said, “I give you every seed-bearing plant on the face of the whole earth and every tree that has fruit with seed in it. summary: For generations, we’ve been told a simple story: fertility just happens. Try hard enough, be patient, and eventually it will work. Instead, they started asking deeper questions — and what they uncovered challenged everything they’d been taught about conception. From the global fertility crisis to sperm count decline, toxin overload, and the outdated notion that fertility is “a women’s issue,” their discoveries revealed a shocking truth: we’ve been approaching fertility all wrong. On this episode of Grounded Wellness, Bethany McDaniel sits down with the WeNatal co-founders to expose what’s really driving modern infertility, why men make up half the fertility equation, and how couples can reclaim their power long before the positive pregnancy test. Together, Bethany, Ronit, and Vita unpack the environmental, biological, and emotional forces shaping fertility today. They explore the dramatic 50-year decline in sperm counts; the rising rates of miscarriage, autoimmune conditions, and hormonal dysfunction; and why “trimester zero” — the 3–6 months before conception — is where the real magic happens. This episode is packed with practical insights for every couple: how to detox your environment, strengthen your mitochondria, choose the right prenatal, regulate stress, improve sleep, and understand the profound genetic impact of men’s health on future children. Above all, it’s a hopeful conversation. Because once you understand the factors influencing fertility, there is so much you can do to improve it. title: 41: How Technology Affects Your Health (and What You Can Do About It) — with August Brice
summary: In a world that’s constantly connected, it’s easy to forget that the same technology keeping us “on” 24/7 might also be quietly wearing us down. From fatigue and anxiety to infertility and insomnia, the devices we depend on could be disrupting our most essential systems — our hormones, sleep, and cellular health. On this episode of Grounded Wellness, Bethany McDaniel sits down with August Brice, founder of Tech Wellness and a leading voice in the movement to help families live healthier digital lives. After developing electromagnetic sensitivity more than 25 years ago, August turned her personal struggle into a mission: to educate, empower, and help others thrive in a high-tech world without fear. August isn’t anti-tech — she’s pro-intention. This conversation reveals the unseen effects of EMF exposure and blue light, how modern devices interfere with our body’s natural rhythms, and the simple, science-backed habits that can protect your energy and your family’s health in a digital world Together, Bethany and August uncover the biological and emotional costs of constant connectivity. They explore how EMFs and wireless radiation disrupt cell signaling and hormone balance, how blue light exposure impacts sleep and melatonin, and why “smart” devices are becoming the next frontier in toxin awareness. But this episode isn’t about fear — it’s about freedom. August shares practical ways to create a tech-safe home, from hardwiring Wi-Fi to building bedtime boundaries with screens. You’ll learn how to detox your environment, reclaim your attention, and use technology the way it was intended: to support, not control, your wellbeing. Discussion Highlights EMFs: The Invisible Stressor 	•	August explains what electromagnetic fields (EMFs) really are — invisible waves of energy from cell phones, Wi-Fi, and Bluetooth devices — and how overexposure can contribute to anxiety, hormone imbalance, oxidative stress, and even fertility issues. Blue Light and Hormone Health 	•	Beyond screen addiction, August details how blue light disrupts circadian rhythm and suppresses melatonin production — even when we wear blue-light glasses. Her nightly rule? No screens after 8 p.m. and amber lighting to mimic natural dusk. The Myth of “Safe” Tech 	•	Just because it’s sold doesn’t mean it’s safe. August reveals that cell phones and Bluetooth devices were never tested for long-term safety, and that modern wearables like earbuds and smartwatches constantly emit wireless radiation — often right against vital organs and glands. Creating a Low-Tech Sanctuary 	•	From hardwiring your home internet to using analog alarm clocks, August shares how to create EMF-safe spaces that promote rest, fertility, and calm. Her simple rule: if you’re not using it, turn it off — especially at night. EMFs and Fertility 	•	Studies show a link between wireless exposure and miscarriage risk, and global sperm counts have dropped by 50% over the past few decades. August urges couples to treat tech toxins like any other environmental toxin — avoidable with awareness and small, consistent action. Digital Detox as a Modern Health Practice 	•	True balance comes from periodic tech fasts — time offline to reconnect with real life. August recommends unplugging daily for at least one hour (no phone, no screens, just presence). It’s not deprivation; it’s nervous system nourishment. Reframing the Narrative Technology doesn’t have to be the enemy — but it shouldn’t be the driver of our lives. The same way we’ve cleaned up our food, filtered our water, and detoxed our skincare, it’s time to detox our digital habits. When we use technology consciously — turning off Wi-Fi at night, hardwiring devices, protecting sleep and hormone health — we move from overstimulation to grounded energy. Awareness becomes our most powerful form of protection.

title: Why Self-Awareness Is More Than Knowing Your Flaws
summary: What does it really mean to be self-aware? In today’s episode, Dr. Robyne unpacks what it really means to see yourself clearly and kindly. She walks through the four archetypes of self-awareness, explains the difference between internal and external awareness, and shares why recognizing your strengths and what’s working well is just as important as noticing where there’s room to grow. title: Ep. 29: How to Decode Your Acne + Heal the Root Cause with Maria Marlowe
summary: If you’ve ever felt like you’re doing everything “right” and still struggling with acne… This episode is for you. In honor of Acne Awareness Month, we brought on a guest who’s not only deeply informed, she’s also been through it herself. Holistic nutritionist and skin health expert Maria Marlowe joins Bethany for a powerful conversation on how to heal your skin from within and why it’s time to rethink everything we’ve been told about acne. After trying every conventional acne treatment out there, from antibiotics to birth control, Maria stumbled across something that changed everything: the healing power of food. Within five months of changing her diet and repairing her gut, her skin completely cleared. Since then, she’s spent over a decade helping others do the same through her Clear Skin Plan and research-backed nutritional guidance. This episode covers so much — from hormone-related breakouts and inflammation to gray hairs, Botox, motherhood, and how the grocery store might secretly be working against your skin. title: Sleep: The Missing Piece of High Performance
summary: In this episode, Dr. Robyne dives into one of the most crucial foundations of our well-being, performance, and long-term success: sleep. While hustle culture glorifies 5 a.m. wake-ups and sleepless nights, research tells a different story—one where most adults need eight hours of quality sleep to truly operate at their best.  She explores the science behind the powerful processes that unfold while we sleep and how a lack of sleep can quietly impact our health, productivity, and decision-making. You’ll also hear practical tips for recharging with naps—like the quick “power nap” or the deeply restorative “heart nap”—along with strategies for building healthy wake-up routines and insights into how your cell phone might be getting in the way of a good night’s sleep.  If you’ve been sacrificing sleep in the name of productivity, feel like you’re running on empty, or want science-backed strategies to dial in your sleep habits, this episode is for you.  Need help navigating emotions?
